7: /// Represents a single attribute with its value, type, and conversion parameters.
8: #[derive(Debug)]
9: pub struct AttributeValue {
10:     pub value: Box<dyn Any + Send + Sync>,
11:     pub value_type: ValueType,
12:     pub value_type_parameters: HashMap<String, String>,
13: }
14: 
15: // Manual implementation of Clone for AttributeValue is tricky because of Box<dyn Any>.
16: // For rollback, we will move the value out and back in, avoiding the need for Clone.
17: 
18: impl AttributeValue {
19:     pub fn new<T: Any + Send + Sync + Clone + 'static>(value: T, value_type: ValueType) -> Self {
20:         AttributeValue {
21:             value: Box::new(value),
22:             value_type,
23:             value_type_parameters: HashMap::new(),
24:         }
25:     }
26: 
27:     pub fn with_parameters(mut self, parameters: HashMap<String, String>) -> Self {
28:         self.value_type_parameters = parameters;
29:         self
30:     }
31: 
32:     pub fn get_value<T: Clone + 'static>(&self) -> Option<T> {
33:         self.value.downcast_ref::<T>().cloned()
34:     }
35: 
36:     pub fn is<T: 'static>(&self) -> bool {
37:         self.value.is::<T>()
38:     }
39: 
40:     pub fn type_id(&self) -> TypeId {
41:         self.value.type_id()
42:     }
43: 
44:     pub fn to_string(&self) -> String {
45:         if let Some(s) = self.value.downcast_ref::<String>() {
46:             s.clone()
47:         } else if let Some(s) = self.value.downcast_ref::<&str>() {
48:             s.to_string()
49:         } else if let Some(i) = self.value.downcast_ref::<i32>() {
50:             i.to_string()
51:         } else if let Some(f) = self.value.downcast_ref::<f64>() {
52:             f.to_string()
53:         } else if let Some(b) = self.value.downcast_ref::<bool>() {
54:             b.to_string()
55:         } else {
56:             format!("{:?}", self.value)
57:         }
58:     }
59: }
60: 
61: /// The main generic data object structure
62: #[derive(Debug)]
63: pub struct GenericDataObject {
64:     attributes: HashMap<String, AttributeValue>,
65:     pub identifier_name: String,
66: }
67: 
68: impl GenericDataObject {
69:     /// Create a new GenericDataObject with a required unique identifier.
70:     pub fn new(identifier_name: &str, id: Option<Uuid>) -> Self {
71:         let mut object = Self {
72:             attributes: HashMap::new(),
73:             identifier_name: identifier_name.to_string(),
74:         };
75: 
76:         let guid = id.unwrap_or_else(Uuid::new_v4);
77:         // Store the UUID as a string for universal compatibility and serialization.
78:         // This initial set should not fail, so we can safely expect success.
79:         object.set(identifier_name, guid.to_string());
80: 
81:         object
82:     }
83: 
84:     pub fn get<T: Clone + 'static + Default>(&mut self, identifier: &str) -> Option<T>
85:     where
86:         T: Any + Clone,
87:     {
88:         let _ = CALLBACK_MANAGER.lock().unwrap().trigger_callbacks(
89:             &EventType::new("BeforeGet"),
90:             self,
91:             &[&identifier],
92:         );
93: 
94:         let attr_value = self.attributes.get(identifier)?;
95: 
96:         let result = if let Some(value) = attr_value.get_value::<T>() {
97:             Some(value)
98:         } else {
99:             let string_value = attr_value.to_string();
100:             let target_type = TypeConverter::infer_value_type(&T::default());
101:             let registry = CONVERSION_REGISTRY.lock().unwrap();
102:             match registry.convert_with_specific_converter(
103:                 &attr_value.value_type.as_str(),
104:                 &target_type.as_str(),
105:                 &string_value,
106:                 &attr_value.value_type_parameters
107:             ) {
108:                 Ok(value) => value.downcast_ref::<T>().cloned(),
109:                 Err(_) => None,
110:             }
111:         };
112: 
113:         let _ = CALLBACK_MANAGER.lock().unwrap().trigger_callbacks(
114:             &EventType::new("AfterGet"),
115:             self,
116:             &[&identifier],
117:         );
118: 
119:         result
120:     }
121: 
122:     /// Set a value in the attributes HashMap
123:     /// Set a value in the attributes HashMap.
124:     /// Returns the old `AttributeValue` if an attribute was replaced, or `None` otherwise.
125:     pub fn set<T: Any + Send + Sync + Clone + 'static>(&mut self, identifier: &str, value: T) -> Option<AttributeValue> {
126:         let value_type = TypeConverter::infer_value_type(&value);
127:         self.set_with_type(identifier, value, value_type, None)
128:     }
129: 
130:     /// Set a value with explicit type and parameters.
131:     /// Returns the old `AttributeValue` if an attribute was replaced, or `None` otherwise.
132:     pub fn set_with_type<T: Any + Send + Sync + Clone + 'static>(
133:         &mut self, 
134:         identifier: &str, 
135:         value: T, 
136:         value_type: ValueType,
137:         parameters: Option<HashMap<String, String>>
138:     ) -> Option<AttributeValue> {
139:         let _ = CALLBACK_MANAGER.lock().unwrap().trigger_callbacks(
140:             &EventType::new("BeforeSet"),
141:             self,
142:             &[&identifier],
143:         );
144: 
145:         let mut new_attr_value = AttributeValue::new(value, value_type);
146:         if let Some(params) = parameters {
147:             new_attr_value = new_attr_value.with_parameters(params);
148:         }
149:         let result = self.attributes.insert(identifier.to_string(), new_attr_value);
150: 
151:         let _ = CALLBACK_MANAGER.lock().unwrap().trigger_callbacks(
152:             &EventType::new("AfterSet"),
153:             self,
154:             &[&identifier],
155:         );
156: 
157:         result
158:     }
